evals.prompt: I'm building a modal dialog component in React for confirming destructive actions (like deleting a project). Make it accessible.
evals.expected_output: Should implement the full accessible modal pattern: role=dialog, aria-modal, focus trap, Escape to close, focus restoration, background inert, and proper labeling.
assertions: Uses role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
Labels the dialog with aria-labelledby pointing to the title
Implements focus trap (Tab cycles within modal)
Moves focus to the first focusable element on open
Returns focus to the trigger element on close
Closes on Escape key
Makes background content inert while open
Destructive button is NOT the default focus (Cancel is safer default)
Uses semantic HTML (button elements, not divs with onClick)

evals.prompt:
Audit this React component for accessibility issues:
navigate(`/project/${id}`)}>
{name}
{status}
{ e.stopPropagation(); onDelete(id); }}> 🗑️
evals.expected_output: Should identify all accessibility issues: clickable div instead of link/button, missing alt on img, color-only status indicator, icon button without label, no keyboard support.
assertions: Identifies clickable div should be an <a> or <Link> (for navigation)
Identifies missing alt attribute on the image
Identifies color-only status indicator (green/red without text or icon)
Identifies delete button as a div — should be <button>
Identifies emoji icon without accessible label (aria-label needed)
Notes that div+onClick has no keyboard support (no Enter/Space handling)
Provides corrected accessible version of the component
Corrected version uses semantic HTML (a, button, img alt)

evals.prompt: We're building a form with email, password, a terms checkbox, and a submit button. The designer wants inline validation that shows errors as you type. Make it accessible.
evals.expected_output: Should implement accessible form with labels, error announcements, aria-invalid, aria-describedby, and proper validation timing.
assertions: Every input has an associated <label> with htmlFor
Error messages use aria-describedby to link to their field
Fields with errors have aria-invalid='true'
Errors are announced to screen readers (role='alert' or aria-live)
Validation is debounced (not on every keystroke)
Checkbox has an associated label (clickable text)
Submit button is a <button type='submit'>
On submit with errors, focus moves to the first field with an error
